subject: UX & Research
title: Voice Control for Motor Access
description: A quick, practical guide to Voice Control for Motor Access.

hook.text: Say 'Click submit.' Your computer clicks it. Voice control lets motor-impaired users navigate by speaking.

buildup.text: Dragon NaturallySpeaking enabled voice control in 1997. Apple Voice Control launched free on macOS in 2019.

discovery.text: Voice control matches spoken words to visible labels. If the label says 'Go' but code says 'Submit,' it fails.

twist.text: Unlabeled icons are impossible to voice-control. Users can't say 'Click hamburger menu' if there's no text.

climax.text: Always match visible labels with code labels. What users see must match what the system understands.

punchline.text: Visible labels must match code labels. Otherwise voice users can't click.

quiz.question: Why do voice control systems fail on some websites?
options: Visible labels don't match code labels, so voice commands can't find the right element
Voice recognition technology is too inaccurate
